question: Recitation Round
answer:
📖 RECITATION ROUND — RULES & INSTRUCTIONS

1. Participants take turns, one after the other, to recite any verse from the Book of Hebrews of their choice.

2. No verse may be repeated. Once a verse has been recited, it cannot be used again by any participant.

3. When it is a participant's turn, they have 5 seconds to begin reciting.

4. A participant is disqualified if they: fail to begin within 5 seconds, recite a verse incorrectly, or repeat a verse already used.

5. Each correct and complete recitation earns 5 marks.

6. The round continues in turns until all participants have been disqualified.

7. No assistance, prompts, or corrections are allowed during recitation.

True or False?: According to Hebrews 3:9, they were not able to enter, because of their disobedience.
answer: FALSE — they were not able to enter because of their unbelief.

question: True or False?: According to Hebrews 8:8, God found fault with the first covenant.
answer: FALSE — But God found fault with the people and said...

question: True or False?: According to Hebrews 10:11, Day after day every priest stands and performs his religious duties; again and again he offers the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
True or False?: According to Hebrews 7:5, the law requires the descendants of Levi who become priests to collect a certain portion from the people because they are not descended from Abraham.
answer: FALSE — the law requires them to collect a tenth from their fellow Israelites, even though they also are descended from Abraham.

question: True or False?: According to Hebrews 13:8, Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
True or False?: According to Hebrews 3:5, Moses was bearing witness to what was spoken by God in the past and present.
answer: FALSE — Moses was faithful as a servant, bearing witness to what would be spoken by God in the future.

question: True or False?: According to Hebrews 7:14, in regard to that tribe (Judah) Moses said nothing about priests.
True or False?: According to Hebrews 1:3, Before the foundations of the world were laid, he (Jesus) sat down at the right hand of the Majesty in heaven.
answer: FALSE — After he had provided purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty in heaven.

question: True or False?: According to Hebrews 2:9, Jesus was made a little lower than the angels for a little while.
answer: TRUE — we see Jesus, who was made lower than the angels for a little while, now crowned with glory and honor because he suffered death, so that by the grace of God he might taste death for everyone.
